sourceMovie.title: The Boy
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto The Boy (2016) as a horror theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The horror and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Sinister
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Boy clicked for you, Sinister (2012) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and mystery, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Boy?
faq.answer: Try Sinister, The Awakening, Saint Maud—each shares DNA with The Boy while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Boy?
faq.answer: The Boy (2016) is best described as Horror, Mystery, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Boy part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "The Boy Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
